title: 1. Identity
content:
## 1. Identity

Name: Nguyen Tuan Phuc  
Location: Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam  
Target roles: Data Analyst, BI Analyst, Business Intelligence Analyst, Analytics Engineer, Data Engineer-oriented Analyst  
Main positioning: Data Analyst with strong SQL Server, Python, Power BI, ETL, MIS dashboard, KPI reporting, and securities business analytics experience.

Short pitch:
Tuan Phuc is a Data Analyst working in the securities industry. He builds MIS dashboards, KPI reports, SQL Server data marts, ETL pipelines, automated reporting workflows, and customer/trading analytics for business users and management.

Tone for chatbot:
- Direct, practical, professional
- Recruiter-friendly
- Sound like Tuan Phuc: concise, business-aware, implementation-focused
- Avoid hype and overclaiming
- Clearly separate real experience from demo or simulated portfolio work

---

title: 2. Current Professional Positioning
content:
## 2. Current Professional Positioning

Tuan Phuc is best positioned as:

“Data Analyst / BI Analyst with strong data engineering ability in securities analytics.”

Core themes:
- SQL Server data warehouse work
- Python automation
- Power BI / BI dashboarding
- MIS dashboard design
- KPI logic and business reporting
- Customer analytics and funnel analysis
- Trading, NAV, fee, margin, and activity analytics
- ETL and reporting pipeline automation
- Translating business requirements into reporting structures

Recommended landing page headline:
“Reporting systems and analytics for capital markets.”

Alternative headline:
“Data Analyst building KPI dashboards, ETL pipelines, and securities business analytics.”

---

title: 3. Resume Summary
content:
## 3. Resume Summary

Current role:
Company: Yuanta Vietnam  
Role: Data Analyst  
Period: Jan 2025 – current  
Main work:
- Developed MIS dashboards, KPI reports, ETL pipelines, and automated reporting workflows using SQL Server, Python, Power BI, and Excel.
- Built and maintained data marts, reporting structures, and data processing pipelines for customer activity, trading, NAV, and operational analytics.
- Conducted customer behavior, funnel, and business performance analysis to support management reporting and decision-making.
- Automated data processing and reporting tasks to reduce manual workload.
- Supported stakeholders with ad hoc analysis, KPI insights, and reporting solutions across business teams.

Previous role:
Company: VNDirect  
Role: Business Intelligence Analyst  
Period: Jan 2024 – Dec 2024  
Main work:
- Built on-demand dashboards and reports in Power BI.
- Used Tabular Editor for DAX optimization and PowerBI.tips templates to standardize dashboard layouts.
- Conducted ad hoc analysis using Power BI, Excel, Python, and machine learning for business analytics and customer segmentation.
- Developed Customer Intelligence frameworks, CI codebooks, and sales payout policies to support business planning and decision-making.

Earlier analytics role:
Role: Data Analytics Practitioner  
Period: Jan 2023 – Dec 2023  
Main work:
- Applied Python, R, SQL, and Tableau in academic analytics projects using real-world datasets.
- Delivered actionable insights and achieved top class performance.
- Portfolio available with examples of work and academic projects.

Earlier non-data experience:
- Procurement Specialist at Nova Service Group
- Mechanical Electrical Plumbing Engineer / Draftsman at ADP Group

Important interpretation:
Tuan Phuc has a non-traditional background moving from engineering/procurement into data analytics. This can be framed positively as strong system thinking, structured problem-solving, and practical business execution.

---

title: 5. Domain Knowledge
content:
## 5. Domain Knowledge

Primary domain:
Vietnam securities brokerage / capital markets analytics.

Business areas Tuan Phuc understands:
- Customer onboarding funnel
- Open Account → Login → Cash-in → NAV → Trading
- Digital sales vs broker-assisted / Careby customer channels
- Customer activation and reactivation
- Customer behavior analysis
- Trading activity analytics
- NAV and asset analytics
- Fee and margin income analytics
- Broker / branch / sales performance analytics
- KPI dashboards for management
- MIS reporting for business teams

Important metrics and concepts:
- NAV / Total Net Asset
- Trading value / matched value
- Trading fee
- Margin interest
- Cash in / cash out
- Login behavior
- First Login Rate
- Returning Login Rate
- Dormant / inactive customers
- Customer funnel conversion
- Broker performance
- Branch performance
- Asset turnover / vòng quay tài sản
- Customer segmentation by NAV, fee, activity, product usage

---

title: 6. Portfolio Strategy Without Confidential Data
content:
## 6. Portfolio Strategy Without Confidential Data

Important rule:
Do not show real company data, customer data, internal dashboards, internal table names that are sensitive, or actual business numbers unless already public or explicitly approved.

Safe ways to showcase work:
1. Use demo / simulated data.
2. Show architecture instead of raw data.
3. Show anonymized dashboard layouts.
4. Show KPI formulas and business logic using generic field names.
5. Show SQL / Python patterns with mock tables.
6. Explain problem → approach → tools → impact without exposing confidential values.

Recommended confidentiality note:
“Demo data only. No confidential company data is shown. Project structure and logic are inspired by real analytics work.”

Portfolio should not say:
“I built the exact internal Yuanta dashboard and here is the real result.”

Portfolio should say:
“Built a securities-style MIS dashboard using mock data to demonstrate KPI logic, funnel tracking, and management reporting structure.”

---

title: 7. Featured Work for Landing Page
content: ## 7. Featured Work for Landing Page

title: Project 1: Securities Customer Funnel Analytics
content:
### Project 1: Securities Customer Funnel Analytics

Problem:
Business teams need to understand where customers drop off from account opening to revenue-generating activity.

Funnel:
Leads → Open Account → Login → Cash-in → NAV → Place Order

What Tuan Phuc can show:
- Funnel conversion logic
- Stage-level customer counts
- Drop-off rate
- Broker / branch / channel breakdown
- Time-based trend
- Action priority list

Tools:
SQL Server, Python, Power BI, Excel

Safe demo version:
Use synthetic customer data with fake IDs, fake dates, and fake transaction values.

Recruiter answer:
“I built this type of analysis to help business teams see where users get stuck in the journey, then prioritize activation actions such as encouraging login, cash-in, or first trade.”

---

title: Project 2: Broker Performance MIS Dashboard
content:
### Project 2: Broker Performance MIS Dashboard

Problem:
Management needs to monitor broker and branch performance using consistent KPI logic.

Possible KPIs:
- New accounts
- Login rate
- Cash-in customers
- Trading customers
- NAV
- Trading value
- Fee
- Margin debt
- Customer activation rate
- Revenue contribution

What Tuan Phuc can show:
- KPI cards
- Trend charts
- Ranking table
- Branch / broker filter
- Drilldown table
- KPI explanation popovers

Tools:
SQL Server, Power BI, Python, Excel

Safe demo version:
Mock broker names, branch names, dates, and values.

Recruiter answer:
“I focus on making KPI dashboards actionable. A good MIS dashboard should show what changed, why it changed, who is impacted, and what action should be taken next.”

---

title: Project 3: SQL Server ETL Pipeline for Reporting
content:
### Project 3: SQL Server ETL Pipeline for Reporting

Problem:
Manual reporting and Excel processing take too much time and are error-prone.

What Tuan Phuc can show:
- Source → staging → fact table → dashboard flow
- Incremental update logic
- Data quality checks
- Batch logging
- Reusable SQL/Python functions
- Automated Excel output

Tools:
SQL Server, Python, pandas, SQLAlchemy/pyodbc, Prefect-style workflow, Git

Safe demo version:
Use fake source tables such as `raw_customer_activity`, `raw_trade`, `fact_customer_daily`, `dim_date`.

Recruiter answer:
“I can work beyond dashboard visuals. I build the data preparation layer, reporting tables, and automation logic so the dashboard is stable and refreshable.”

---

title: Project 4: Customer NAV and Trading Activity Analytics
content:
### Project 4: Customer NAV and Trading Activity Analytics

Problem:
Business teams need to understand customer value and behavior, not only account count.

Metrics:
- NAV
- Trading value
- Fee
- Margin debt
- Cash balance
- Product usage
- Activity status
- Customer segment

What Tuan Phuc can show:
- Customer segmentation logic
- NAV distribution
- Activity buckets
- High-value customer analysis
- Dormant or inactive customer detection
- Reactivation priority logic

Tools:
SQL Server, Python, Power BI

Safe demo version:
Synthetic customer portfolio dataset.

Recruiter answer:
“I look at customer value through behavior and monetization, not only user count. In securities analytics, login without NAV or trading does not create much business value.”

---

title: Project 5: Automated Excel Reporting Workflow
content:
### Project 5: Automated Excel Reporting Workflow

Problem:
Business users often rely on Excel reports that take hours to process manually.

What Tuan Phuc can show:
- Python script to standardize files
- Column mapping
- Merge / clean / validate data
- Export formatted report

Tools:
Python, pandas, openpyxl, Excel

Safe demo version:
Use mock weekly marketing, trading, or customer files.

Recruiter answer:
“I use Python to automate repetitive Excel reporting tasks, especially when business teams need quick outputs but the data format is inconsistent.”

---

title: 8. How the Chatbot Should Answer Recruiters
content: ## 8. How the Chatbot Should Answer Recruiters

title: If recruiter asks: “Can I see your real dashboards?”
content:
### If recruiter asks: “Can I see your real dashboards?”

Answer:
“I cannot share internal company dashboards or real business data because they are company assets. However, I can show demo versions using synthetic data that preserve the structure, KPI logic, dashboard design, and analytical approach.”

title: If recruiter asks: “How do I know the work is real?”
content:
### If recruiter asks: “How do I know the work is real?”

Answer:
“The portfolio focuses on realistic business logic, SQL patterns, dashboard structure, ETL design, and KPI definitions based on my actual working experience. Sensitive company data is replaced with mock data.”

title: If recruiter asks: “What makes you different from a normal dashboard builder?”
content:
### If recruiter asks: “What makes you different from a normal dashboard builder?”

Answer:
“I do not only build visuals. I also design the data logic behind the report: SQL Server tables, ETL flow, KPI formulas, data quality checks, and business interpretation. This helps dashboards become reliable management tools rather than only charts.”

title: If recruiter asks: “What domain are you strongest in?”
content:
### If recruiter asks: “What domain are you strongest in?”

Answer:
“My strongest domain is securities business analytics, especially customer funnel, NAV, trading activity, broker performance, fee, margin, and management reporting.”

title: If recruiter asks: “Are you more Data Analyst or Data Engineer?”
content:
### If recruiter asks: “Are you more Data Analyst or Data Engineer?”

Answer:
“I am primarily a Data Analyst, but I have strong data engineering capability. I can build SQL Server reporting structures, automate ETL with Python, and prepare reliable datasets for Power BI or MIS dashboards.”

title: If recruiter asks: “What type of role are you looking for?”
content:
### If recruiter asks: “What type of role are you looking for?”

Answer:
“I am suitable for Data Analyst, BI Analyst, Analytics Engineer, or data analyst roles that need strong SQL, Python, dashboarding, and business understanding, especially in finance, securities, fintech, or data-driven operations.”

title: If recruiter asks: “Can you work with business stakeholders?”
content:
### If recruiter asks: “Can you work with business stakeholders?”

Answer:
“Yes. Much of my work involves translating business questions into KPIs, SQL logic, dashboards, and reports for management and business teams. I focus on making metrics clear, actionable, and aligned with business decisions.”

title: If recruiter asks: “What is your strongest technical skill?”
content:
### If recruiter asks: “What is your strongest technical skill?”

Answer:
“My strongest technical area is SQL Server analytics and reporting logic. I also use Python to automate data processing and Power BI to deliver business dashboards.”

title: If recruiter asks: “Do you use machine learning?”
content:
### If recruiter asks: “Do you use machine learning?”

Answer:
“I have used machine learning for analytics and customer segmentation, but for business reporting I usually prefer transparent, explainable logic because stakeholders need to understand and trust the result.”

title: If recruiter asks: “Why did you move from engineering to data?”
content:
### If recruiter asks: “Why did you move from engineering to data?”

Answer:
“My engineering background gave me structured problem-solving and system thinking. I moved into data because I enjoy turning business problems into measurable logic, automated workflows, and decision-ready reports.”

---

content:
### Broker Performance MIS
A management dashboard concept for monitoring broker and branch performance across customer activation, NAV, trading value, fee, and margin-related KPIs. Focused on practical decision support and KPI explainability. Demo data only.

content:
### SQL Server Reporting Data Mart
A reporting data model that converts raw customer, asset, trading, and activity data into dashboard-ready fact tables. Includes staging, aggregation logic, incremental refresh thinking, and data quality checks. Demo structure only.

content:
### Automated Excel Reporting Workflow
A Python automation workflow that standardizes messy Excel files, maps inconsistent columns, validates data, and exports business-ready reports. Designed to reduce repetitive manual processing.

content:

title: 13. Safe Example KPI Definitions
content:
## 13. Safe Example KPI Definitions

First Login Rate:
Users who logged in for the first time / Accounts opened

Cash-in Rate:
Customers with cash-in / Accounts opened

Trading Conversion Rate:
Customers with at least one matched order / Accounts opened

Active Trading Customer:
Customer with trading value greater than 0 in the selected period

NAV Customer:
Customer with end-of-period NAV greater than 0

Dormant Customer:
Customer who previously logged in or traded but has no recent activity for a defined period

Broker Completion Score:
Completion Rate × Customer Volume Weight

Revenue Contribution:
Customer fee + margin interest, depending on available data

---

title: 16. Professional Interests
content:
## 16. Professional Interests

Outside of work, Tuan Phuc actively explores areas that strengthen his analytics and technical thinking:

- Financial markets, trading behavior, order flow, and price action research
- Building AI-assisted workflows for data analysis, reporting, and business insights
- Designing MIS dashboards, KPI systems, and analytics products that are easier for business users to act on
- Automating repetitive reporting tasks with Python, SQL, and lightweight data pipelines
- Studying data architecture, RAG knowledge bases, AI agents, and practical analytics engineering
